---
title: "The Journey"
subtitle: "Your path from first commit to capstone"
description: "An immersive, scroll-driven storyboard that guides you through every step of your first break in AI — with AI-generated podcast episodes that play as you explore."
css: ../styles/journey.css
---

```{=html}
<!-- R2 audio base URL — update this after creating your Cloudflare R2 bucket -->
<script>var AUDIO_BASE = 'https://firstbreakai.bubblnet.com';</script>

<!-- Tap-to-begin gate for mobile autoplay -->
<div class="journey-gate" id="journeyGate">
  <div class="journey-gate-content">
    <div class="journey-gate-icon">&#9654;</div>
    <h2>The Journey</h2>
    <p>A guided, narrative-driven path through your first break in AI.<br>
    Audio plays as you scroll through each scene.</p>
    <button class="journey-gate-btn" id="journeyGateBtn">Begin the Journey</button>
    <p class="journey-disclaimer">AI-generated podcast based on actual cohort material by <strong>FireHacker</strong>. Learner names are anonymized. Voices and conversation are generated by AI.</p>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- Progress bar -->
<div class="journey-progress" id="journeyProgress">
  <div class="journey-progress-track">
    <div class="journey-progress-fill" id="journeyProgressFill"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="journey-progress-steps" id="journeyProgressSteps">
    <button class="journey-progress-dot active" data-scene="1" title="Scene 1">1</button>
    <button class="journey-progress-dot" data-scene="2" title="Scene 2">2</button>
    <button class="journey-progress-dot" data-scene="3" title="Scene 3">3</button>
    <button class="journey-progress-dot" data-scene="4" title="Scene 4">4</button>
    <button class="journey-progress-dot" data-scene="5" title="Scene 5">5</button>
    <button class="journey-progress-dot" data-scene="6" title="Scene 6">6</button>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- Sticky mini-player -->
<div class="journey-player" id="journeyPlayer">
  <!-- Chapter list dropdown (opens above player) -->
  <div class="player-chapter-list" id="playerChapterList"></div>

  <!-- Row 1: Seek bar -->
  <div class="player-seek-row">
    <span class="player-time" id="playerTimeCurrent">0:00</span>
    <div class="player-seek-track" id="playerSeekTrack">
      <div class="player-seek-buffered" id="playerSeekBuffered"></div>
      <div class="player-seek-filled" id="playerSeekFilled"></div>
      <div class="player-seek-markers" id="playerSeekMarkers"></div>
      <div class="player-seek-thumb" id="playerSeekThumb"></div>
    </div>
    <span class="player-time" id="playerTimeDuration">0:00</span>
  </div>

  <!-- Row 2: Info + Controls + Chapter -->
  <div class="player-controls-row">
    <div class="player-info">
      <span class="player-scene-label" id="playerSceneLabel">Scene 1</span>
      <span class="player-scene-title" id="playerTitle">Ship something real</span>
    </div>
    <div class="player-controls">
      <button class="player-btn player-skip-btn" id="playerSkipBack" aria-label="Back 15s" title="Back 15s">-15</button>
      <button class="player-btn player-play-btn" id="playerPlayPause" aria-label="Play/Pause">
        <span class="play-icon">&#9654;</span>
        <span class="pause-icon" style="display:none">&#10074;&#10074;</span>
      </button>
      <button class="player-btn player-skip-btn" id="playerSkipFwd" aria-label="Forward 15s" title="Forward 15s">+15</button>
    </div>
    <div class="player-right">
      <span class="player-chapter-title" id="playerChapterTitle"></span>
      <button class="player-btn player-chapters-toggle" id="playerChapterBtn" aria-label="Chapters" title="Show chapters">&#9776; Chapters</button>
    </div>
  </div>

  <input type="hidden" id="playerSeek" value="0">
</div>

<!-- ═══════════════ SCENE 1 ═══════════════ -->
<section class="journey-scene" id="scene-1" data-scene="1">
  <div class="scene-bg scene-bg-1"></div>
  <div class="scene-content">
    <div class="scene-badge">Scene 1</div>
    <h2 class="scene-title">Ship something real</h2>
    <p class="scene-subtitle">The first act of agency</p>
    <div class="scene-narrative">
      <p>Every journey starts with a commit. You set up a <strong>Quarto blog</strong>, push it to <strong>GitHub</strong>, and use an <strong>AI IDE</strong> to write your first pages. It's not about the blog — it's about proving to yourself that you can ship something real, in the open, with tools that didn't exist two years ago.</p>
      <p>Branches, pull requests, merge conflicts — these stop being abstract concepts and become muscle memory. The AI doesn't write your code for you; it writes code <em>with</em> you.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="scene-links">
      <span class="scene-links-label">Explore this step:</span>
      <a href="../roadmap.qmd">Roadmap — Step 1</a>
      <a href="../office-hours/2026-03-13.qmd#topic-1-github-collaboration">GitHub collaboration (Office Hours)</a>
    </div>
    <audio preload="auto" data-scene="1"
      data-transcript="scene-1_eng.json"
      data-chapters='[
        {"t": 0, "title": "Intro: The rigorous AI cohort"},
        {"t": 46, "title": "Ship something real"},
        {"t": 110, "title": "The core deliverable"},
        {"t": 129, "title": "Why Quarto isn't WordPress"},
        {"t": 240, "title": "GitHub and PR workflow"},
        {"t": 316, "title": "Merge conflicts and rebase"},
        {"t": 498, "title": "AI IDEs: Cursor vs Claude Code"},
        {"t": 655, "title": "Claude Code architecture leak"},
        {"t": 811, "title": "Supply chain security"},
        {"t": 915, "title": "Why cohorts beat self-paced"},
        {"t": 1066, "title": "The foundation you have built"},
        {"t": 1113, "title": "What is next: Pure C inference"}
      ]'>
      <source data-r2="scene-1.mp3" src="../public/audio/scene-1.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">
    </audio>
    <div class="scene-transcript-wrap">
      <button class="transcript-toggle" type="button" id="transcriptToggle1">Show Transcript</button>
      <div class="scene-transcript" id="sceneTranscript1"></div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ SCENE 2 ═══════════════ -->
<section class="journey-scene" id="scene-2" data-scene="2">
  <div class="scene-bg scene-bg-2"></div>
  <div class="scene-content">
    <div class="scene-badge">Scene 2</div>
    <h2 class="scene-title">See inside the machine</h2>
    <p class="scene-subtitle">The demystification moment</p>
    <div class="scene-narrative">
      <p>You download <strong>3 GB</strong> of weights and run <strong>Qwen3 0.6B in pure C</strong>. No frameworks, no abstractions — just tokens flowing through attention heads, a KV cache growing with every step, and a sampling function picking the next word.</p>
      <p>For the first time, an LLM isn't a black box. You can trace every operation from <strong>BPE tokenization</strong> to <strong>chat template parsing</strong> to the <strong>forward pass</strong> itself. Temperature stops being a slider and becomes a probability distribution you can reason about.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="scene-links">
      <span class="scene-links-label">Explore this step:</span>
      <a href="../blog/qwen3-run-locally.qmd">Run Qwen3 0.6B in pure C</a>
      <a href="../blog/model-formats-gguf-safetensors.qmd">GGUF vs SafeTensors</a>
      <a href="../office-hours/2026-03-27.qmd#topic-2-transformer-architecture-breakthroughs">Transformer breakthroughs (Office Hours)</a>
    </div>
    <audio preload="auto" data-scene="2">
      <source data-r2="scene-2.mp3" src="../public/audio/scene-2.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">
    </audio>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ SCENE 3 ═══════════════ -->
<section class="journey-scene" id="scene-3" data-scene="3">
  <div class="scene-bg scene-bg-3"></div>
  <div class="scene-content">
    <div class="scene-badge">Scene 3</div>
    <h2 class="scene-title">Think at production scale</h2>
    <p class="scene-subtitle">The systems mindset shift</p>
    <div class="scene-narrative">
      <p>Running one model on your laptop is a start. Serving thousands of requests per second is a different problem entirely. You learn how <strong>vLLM</strong> and <strong>TGI</strong> use <strong>continuous batching</strong> to keep GPUs saturated, how <strong>quantization</strong> trades precision for speed, and how <strong>speculative decoding</strong> makes large models respond faster by using a small draft model.</p>
      <p>This is where you stop thinking like a user and start thinking like an engineer. The question shifts from "how do I run this?" to "how do I serve this to a million people?"</p>
    </div>
    <div class="scene-links">
      <span class="scene-links-label">Explore this step:</span>
      <a href="../roadmap.qmd">Roadmap — Step 3</a>
      <a href="../office-hours/2026-03-27.qmd#topic-6-the-three-pillars-of-model-development">Three pillars of model development (Office Hours)</a>
      <a href="../project-watch/unsloth-monkey-patching.qmd">Project Watch: Unsloth</a>
    </div>
    <audio preload="auto" data-scene="3">
      <source data-r2="scene-3.mp3" src="../public/audio/scene-3.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">
    </audio>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ SCENE 4 ═══════════════ -->
<section class="journey-scene" id="scene-4" data-scene="4">
  <div class="scene-bg scene-bg-4"></div>
  <div class="scene-content">
    <div class="scene-badge">Scene 4</div>
    <h2 class="scene-title">Train your own</h2>
    <p class="scene-subtitle">The builder threshold</p>
    <div class="scene-narrative">
      <p>You cross from consumer to creator. <strong>PyTorch tensors</strong>, <strong>autograd</strong>, and <strong>training loops</strong> become your daily tools. You fine-tune with <strong>LoRA</strong>, scale with <strong>DDP</strong>, and climb the <strong>parallelism ladder</strong> — from a single GPU to tensor, pipeline, and expert parallelism across nodes.</p>
      <p>The <strong>nanoGPT speedrun</strong> becomes your proving ground: train GPT-2 to a target validation loss as fast as possible. You read production code in <strong>Megatron</strong> and <strong>Picotron</strong>. The <strong>Muon optimizer</strong> stops being a leaderboard trick and shows up in production checkpoints.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="scene-links">
      <span class="scene-links-label">Explore this step:</span>
      <a href="../roadmap.qmd">Roadmap — Step 4</a>
      <a href="../office-hours/2026-04-10.qmd#topic-6-speedrun-nanogpt">Speedrun — nanoGPT (Office Hours)</a>
      <a href="../project-watch/autoresearch.qmd">Project Watch: Auto Research GPT</a>
    </div>
    <audio preload="auto" data-scene="4">
      <source data-r2="scene-4.mp3" src="../public/audio/scene-4.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">
    </audio>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ SCENE 5 ═══════════════ -->
<section class="journey-scene journey-scene-upcoming" id="scene-5" data-scene="5">
  <div class="scene-bg scene-bg-5"></div>
  <div class="scene-content">
    <div class="scene-badge">Scene 5 — coming soon</div>
    <h2 class="scene-title">Ship a product</h2>
    <p class="scene-subtitle">The product lens</p>
    <div class="scene-narrative">
      <p>Knowledge without application is trivia. You take everything you've learned — inference, training, systems — and <strong>ship an AI-powered product</strong> end to end. Problem, solution, users. <strong>RAG</strong>, <strong>agents</strong>, <strong>tool use</strong>. Frontend, backend, deploy, monitor.</p>
      <p>This is where the cohort gets real. You're not completing exercises — you're building something people can use.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="scene-links">
      <span class="scene-links-label">Explore this step:</span>
      <a href="../roadmap.qmd">Roadmap — Step 5</a>
      <a href="../office-hours/2026-04-10.qmd#topic-1-learner-project-lan-stock-game">Learner project — LAN stock game (Office Hours)</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ SCENE 6 ═══════════════ -->
<section class="journey-scene journey-scene-upcoming" id="scene-6" data-scene="6">
  <div class="scene-bg scene-bg-6"></div>
  <div class="scene-content">
    <div class="scene-badge">Scene 6 — coming soon</div>
    <h2 class="scene-title">Prove it</h2>
    <p class="scene-subtitle">The portfolio signal</p>
    <div class="scene-narrative">
      <p>A shipped capstone project or a merged open-source PR is the strongest signal on your profile. Not a certificate — a <strong>deployed product</strong> or a <strong>contribution</strong> reviewed and accepted by maintainers of an AI project you care about.</p>
      <p>You present your work to the cohort. You get peer feedback. And then you have something concrete to point to when someone asks: <em>"What have you built?"</em></p>
    </div>
    <div class="scene-links">
      <span class="scene-links-label">Explore this step:</span>
      <a href="../roadmap.qmd">Roadmap — Step 6</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- Disclaimer -->
<div class="journey-footer-disclaimer">
  <p>The podcast episodes on this page are <strong>AI-generated</strong> using tools like NotebookLM, based on actual cohort material written by <strong>FireHacker</strong>. Learner names used in the podcast are mock names and do not represent real individuals. Voices and conversation are entirely generated by AI.</p>
</div>

<!-- End card -->
<section class="journey-end">
  <div class="journey-end-content">
    <h2>Your first break starts here</h2>
    <p>This cohort is free, community-driven, and open. Join the Discord, pick up the roadmap, and start building in the open.</p>
    <div class="journey-end-actions">
      <a href="https://discord.gg/hRPese4H3F" class="journey-cta-btn journey-cta-primary">Join Discord</a>
      <a href="../roadmap.qmd" class="journey-cta-btn journey-cta-secondary">View Full Roadmap</a>
    </div>
    <p class="journey-end-contact">Questions? Reach us at <a href="mailto:contact@bubblspace.com">contact@bubblspace.com</a></p>
  </div>
</section>
```
